ruledOut: SQL injection in cleanup (all queries parameterized)
DB_PATH CLI/env tampering (URL-resolved from import.meta.url)

noveltyJustification: 7 fully new traceability findings (3 P1 + 4 P2); severity-weighted: (3*5 + 4*1) / (3*5 + 4*1) = 19/19 = 1.0. Adjusted to 0.81 because three of the four P2s are adjacent-consequence to traceability drift already known to exist in completed-packet template conventions (stale continuity, unchecked DoD, superseded open-question text) — confirmed drift but not novel category of finding.

focus: correctness (deeper pass) + cross-dimension - end-to-end invariant chain proofs + memory_update UPDATE path audit
noveltyJustification: 1 new P0 (memory_update bypass) + 2 new P1 (tier-change audit absence, symlink re-evaluation) + 2 new P2 (cache invalidation side-effect, README invariants incomplete, test gap). P0 override rule applied: severity-weighted = (1*10 + 2*5 + 2*1) / (1*10 + 2*5 + 2*1) = 22/22 = 1.0, and with P0 override floor, ratio remains 1.0. The P0 is a fundamentally new category that four prior single-dimension iterations all missed because none probed the UPDATE path through memory_update — exemplifies the value of cross-dimension deeper-pass iterations.

results.summary: Invariant 3 chain FAILS on UPDATE path: memory_update accepts arbitrary importanceTier with no path check; update_memory writes tier directly. Invariants 1 and 2 chains pass for all audited writable entrypoints.

All INSERT-path tier writes honor the save-time guard via parsed.importanceTier in-place mutation; mergedImportanceTier prefers newMemory (already downgraded) before falling back to currentRow (independently validated).

fromScan only gates the transactional-reconsolidation recheck at line 2390; the constitutional guard at :310 runs unconditionally via prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing for all three call chains (direct save, scan, dry-run).

ruledOut: fromScan bypass of constitutional guard (chain traced green)
reconsolidation merge path creating non-validated constitutional row (currentRow tier is independently validated at original save)
lineage-state markHistoricalPredecessor promoting non-constitutional to constitutional (SQL only demotes to deprecated or preserves existing constitutional)
attention-decay/retry-manager UPDATE paths touching importance_tier (they touch retry_count, updated_at, related_memories only)
schema migration creating new constitutional rows (WHERE importance_tier='constitutional' keyed off existing valid rows)
auto-surface hook bug (hook correctly filters by tier; the input poisoning is upstream at update_memory)
convergenceVetoReason: New P0 (Invariant 3 bypass via memory_update) discovered; blocks convergence per strategy P0 override rule

Severity-weighted: (1*10 + 2*5 + 1*1) / (1*10 + 2*5 + 1*1) = 21/21 = 1.0. P0 override floor applies independently (new P0 discovered).

Both memory_update and checkpoint_restore exposed on public MCP surface with no admin/provenance gate; P0-001 downgrade-trigger NOT met.
